name: databricks
label: Databricks
description: Query an Azure Databricks Cluster table in a catalog's schema (database). Uses Databricks SQL Connector for Python.

fields.title: Server Hostname
fields.name: databricks_server_hostname
fields.description: The server hostname of the all-purpose compute or SQL warehouse. You can get this from the Server Hostname value in the Advanced Options > JDBC/ODBC tab for your all-purpose compute or Connection Details tab for your SQL warehouse.
fields.tooltip: The server hostname of the all-purpose compute or SQL warehouse. You can get this from the Server Hostname value in the Advanced Options > JDBC/ODBC tab for your all-purpose compute or Connection Details tab for your SQL warehouse.

fields.title: HTTP Path
fields.name: databricks_http_path
fields.description: The HTTP path of the all-purpose compute or the SQL warehouse. You can get this from the HTTP Path value in the Advanced Options > JDBC/ODBC tab for your all-purpose compute or the Connection Details tab for your SQL warehouse.
fields.tooltip: The HTTP path of the all-purpose compute or the SQL warehouse. You can get this from the HTTP Path value in the Advanced Options > JDBC/ODBC tab for your all-purpose compute or the Connection Details tab for your SQL warehouse.

fields.title: Client ID
fields.name: databricks_client_id
fields.description: Create a Databricks service principal in your Databricks workspace, and create an OAuth secret for that service principal. This will be the service principal's UUID or Application ID value.
fields.tooltip: Create a Databricks service principal in your Databricks workspace, and create an OAuth secret for that service principal. This will be the service principal's UUID or Application ID value.

fields.title: Client Secret
fields.name: databricks_client_secret
fields.description: Create a Databricks service principal in your Databricks workspace, and create an OAuth secret for that service principal. This will be the Secret value for the service principal's OAuth secret.
fields.tooltip: Create a Databricks service principal in your Databricks workspace, and create an OAuth secret for that service principal. This will be the Secret value for the service principal's OAuth secret.

operations.title: Run Query
operations.description: Executes a query on the database. You can execute any valid SQL query such as Create, Update, Delete, Insert or Select.

parameters.title: Query String
parameters.description: Query that you want to trigger on the database. You can perform all sorts of operations such as Create, Update, Delete, Insert, or Select, on the database using this query.
parameters.name: query_string
parameters.tooltip: Query that you want to trigger on the database. You can perform all sorts of operations such as Create, Update, Delete, Insert, or Select, on the database using this query.

playbook_collections.name: Sample - Databricks - 1.0.0
playbook_collections.description: Run SELECT Query on Databricks Cluster
